summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a blistering early‑'80s show with standout renditions of Althea, China>Rider, Shakedown Street and a monster Brent solo on Big River.
review: Both reviewers rave about the crystal‑clear soundboard source and praise the performance as one of the strongest of 1981. Highlights include scorching versions of Althea, China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, Shakedown Street, He's Gone and a jaw‑dropping Brent Mydland solo on Big River (plus a killer Little Red Rooster), while Jerry Garcia’s fast‑ahead guitar work and soulful vocals are singled out as exceptional.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently five‑star reviews emphasize both superb soundboard quality and an outstanding musical performance, with multiple specific song praises and no noted flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the recording as crystal‑clear, well‑balanced and “great,” with no mention of dropouts or muddiness.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as full‑tilt, reminiscent of the 1966 era, with tight transitions and high energy throughout; the overall flow is praised as consistently strong.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s fast‑ahead lead work, soulful vocal on Althea, aggressive soloing and surprising slide lick at the end of Little Red Rooster are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s slide playing is noted as less stellar compared to Garcia; otherwise not a focus.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives high praise for inventive solos on Big River and Little Red Rooster, with his tone and “monster growl” singled out.
